meaning: vt.宣告无罪,脱卸义务和责任,还清(债务)
examples: Will the court acquit him of the crime?
I acquit Edward of all essential misconduct.
The judge directed the jury to acquit all the defendants.
The panelists could throw up their hands and acquit Avandia on the basis of reasonable doubt.
If the prosecution fails to prove even one element, a jury is obliged to acquit.
